publications.ref: Kim MS, Pinto SM, Getnet D, Nirujogi RS, Manda SS, Chaerkady R, Madugundu AK, Kelkar DS, Isserlin R, Jain S, Thomas JK, Muthusamy B, Leal-Rojas P, Kumar P, Sahasrabuddhe NA, Balakrishnan L, Advani J, George B, Renuse S, Selvan LD, Patil AH, Nanjappa V, Radhakrishnan A, Prasad S, Subbannayya T, Raju R, Kumar M, Sreenivasamurthy SK, Marimuthu A, Sathe GJ, Chavan S, Datta KK, Subbannayya Y, Sahu A, Yelamanchi SD, Jayaram S, Rajagopalan P, Sharma J, Murthy KR, Syed N, Goel R, Khan AA, Ahmad S, Dey G, Mudgal K, Chatterjee A, Huang TC, Zhong J, Wu X, Shaw PG, Freed D, Zahari MS, Mukherjee KK, Shankar S, Mahadevan A, Lam H, Mitchell CJ, Shankar SK, Satishchandra P, Schroeder JT, Sirdeshmukh R, Maitra A, Leach SD, Drake CG, Halushka MK, Prasad TS, Hruban RH, Kerr CL, Bader GD, Iacobuzio-Donahue CA, Gowda H, Pandey A. A draft map of the human proteome. Nature. 2014 May 29;509(7502):575-81

Pinto SM, Manda SS, Kim MS, Taylor K, Selvan LD, Balakrishnan L, Subbannayya T, Yan F, Prasad TS, Gowda H, Lee C, Hancock WS, Pandey A. Functional annotation of proteome encoded by human chromosome 22. J Proteome Res. 2014 Mar 26

Characteristics[Organism].evidence: chromosome-centric human proteome project (C-HPP) initiative, we report our progress on the annotation of chromosome 22

Characteristics[OrganismPart].evidence: On the basis of proteomic profiling of 30 histologically normal tissues and cells using high-resolution mass spectrometry

Characteristics[CellType].evidence: On the basis of proteomic profiling of 30 histologically normal tissues and cells using high-resolution mass spectrometry

Characteristics[MaterialType].evidence: we show protein evidence of 367 genes on chromosome 22

Characteristics[Disease].reasoning: 論文で言及されているが、実際の実験サンプルが疾患由来かは明記されていない。'histologically normal tissues'という記述から健常組織の可能性が高い
Characteristics[Disease].needs: 実験に使用したサンプルが疾患由来か健常由来かの明確な記述

Characteristics[Label].reasoning: 特定の標識法（TMT、SILAC、iTRAQ等）の記述がないため、label-free proteomicsの可能性
Characteristics[Label].needs: Methods sectionでの標識法に関する具体的記述

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: ヒト染色体22のプロテオーム解析研究で、30種類の組織正常組織・細胞を高分解能質量分析で解析。疾患関連の記述はあるが実験サンプルは健常組織と推定。標識法や具体的な組織・細胞種の詳細は不明で、Methods sectionでの確認が必要。

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: subjected to in-gel digestion using trypsin ... subjected to in-solution trypsin digestion

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: reduction and alkylation with DTT and IAA, respectively

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: reduction and alkylation with DTT and IAA, respectively ... lysis buffer containing 4% SDS, 100 mM DTT

Characteristics[Label].evidence: 標識試薬（TMT、iTRAQ、SILAC等）の記述が一切ないこと、および前ステップの推論が今回のMethodsで確認された

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: Carbamidomethylation of cysteine was specified as fixed modification

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: oxidation of methionine ... included as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: acetylation of protein N-termini ... included as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].3.evidence: cyclization of N-terminal glutamine were included as variable modifications

Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: loaded on an XBridge C18, 5 μm 250 × 4.6 mm column ... reconstituted in bRPLC solvent A (10 mM TEABC, pH 8.5)

Comment[NumberOfFractions].evidence: Ninety-six fractions were collected which were then concatenated to 24 fractions

Comment[Separation].evidence: bRPLC using XBridge C18 column with 10 mM TEABC buffer pH 8.5

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: サンプル調製の詳細が確定：トリプシン消化、DTT還元、IAA（ヨードアセトアミド）アルキル化でlabel-free解析。カルバミドメチル化（固定修飾）、メチオニン酸化・N末端アセチル化・N末端グルタミン環化（可変修飾）を設定。bRPLC（塩基性逆相LC）で96画分を24画分に集約してフラクショネーション。濃縮や枯渇処理は実施されていない。

Comment[Instrument].evidence: Peptide samples from in-gel digestion and bRPLC fractionation were analyzed on LTQ-Orbitrap Velos and LTQ-Orbitrap Elite mass spectrometers (Thermo Electron, Bremen, Germany)

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: were fragmented using higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD)

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: Data-dependent acquisition with full scans in the 350–1800 m/z range were carried out

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: interfaced with an Easy-nLC II nanoflow liquid chromatography system

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: detected at a mass resolution of 15 000 and 30 000 at 400 m/z in Velos and Elite, respectively

Comment[CollisionEnergy].evidence: were fragmented using higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD) with 35% normalized collision energy

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: The peptide and fragment mass tolerance was set to 10 ppm and 0.05 Da for fragment ions

Comment[FragmentMassTolerance].evidence: The peptide and fragment mass tolerance was set to 10 ppm and 0.05 Da for fragment ions

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: Enzyme specificity was set as trypsin with maximum of two missed cleavages allowed

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: using a linear gradient of 7–30% solvent B (0.1% formic acid in 95% acetonitrile) over 60 min

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: Peptides were resolved on an analytical column (75 μm × 10 cm) at a flow rate of 350 nL/min

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: LC-MS/MS装置・パラメータの詳細が全て確定：LTQ-Orbitrap Velos/EliteでHCDフラグメンテーション、DDA取得、35%衝突エネルギー、プリカーサー10ppm・フラグメント0.05Da質量許容誤差、トリプシン2ミスクリ許容、60分グラジエント・350nL/min流速で分析。質量分析条件は完全に確定し、次ステップではデータ解析・同定パラメータの詳細確認が必要。

Comment[FractionIdentifier].evidence: Raw file names contain fraction identifiers in the format '_f##' (e.g., f07, f09, f13, f21, f36). From methods: 'Ninety-six fractions were collected which were then concatenated to 24 fractions'
Comment[FractionIdentifier].source: filename pattern analysis and methods section

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: 30 different tissue/cell types identified from raw file names: Adult_Adrenalgland, Adult_Bcells, Adult_CD4Tcells, Adult_CD8Tcells, Adult_Colon, Adult_Esophagus, Adult_Frontalcortex, Adult_Gallbladder, Adult_Heart, Adult_Kidney, Adult_Liver, Adult_Lung, Adult_Monocytes, Adult_NKcells, Adult_Ovary, Adult_Pancreas, Adult_Platelets, Adult_Prostate, Adult_Rectum, Adult_Retina, Adult_Spinalcord, Adult_Testis, Adult_Urinarybladder, Fetal_Brain, Fetal_Gut, Fetal_Heart, Fetal_Liver, Fetal_Ovary, Fetal_Placenta, Fetal_Testis. Consistent with methods: 'Histologically normal human organs, fetal tissues, and hematopoietic cells'

Comment[NumberOfFractions].evidence: From methods section: 'Ninety-six fractions were collected which were then concatenated to 24 fractions'. Raw file fraction identifiers range from f01 to f38, but concentrated into 24 fractions as stated.

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].reasoning: Methods states 'equal amounts from each of the donors was pooled for further fractionation', suggesting pooled biological samples rather than individual replicates. No clear biological replicate numbering in file names.
Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].needs: Confirmation whether pooled samples constitute single biological replicate or if multiple donors were processed separately

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.reasoning: Methods indicates samples were pooled: 'equal amounts from each of the donors was pooled', suggesting single pooled biological replicate per tissue/cell type rather than multiple separate replicates
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.needs: Clarification on experimental design - pooled vs separate biological replicates

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.reasoning: Most tissues appear once per fractionation method (Gel vs bRP), but some have multiple entries. Need to determine if multiple files per tissue represent technical replicates or different fractionation approaches.
Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.needs: Analysis of whether multiple files per tissue/method combination represent technical replicates

FactorValue[FractionIdentifier].reasoning: Raw files contain fraction identifiers (_f##), and methods describes fractionation process. This could be treated as a factor value rather than just a comment.
FactorValue[FractionIdentifier].needs: Determination of whether fraction identifier should be factor value or comment

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: Confirmed 30 different tissue/cell types with fraction-based separation (24 fractions from original 96). Raw file names contain clear fraction identifiers (_f##) but biological vs technical replicate structure remains unclear due to pooled sample preparation described in methods. Most factor values are not applicable as this is an observational proteomics study without experimental treatments.
